screenshot_notes: Fresh screenshots captured at 15:45 UTC at 1280x800 and 375x812. Site is live and fully rendered. Vibe: Crushing It, LEGEND 🔥. All 5 workers at desks, Sofia showing red error ring. Window element top-right is a jarring blown-out white/light-blue rectangle against dark floor — confirmed again. Large wander-orbit circle visible lower-left quadrant (looks like a stray CSS artifact or mispositioned conference table). ShortcutToast visible bottom-right on desktop. On mobile, ShortcutToast renders as plain unstyled text BELOW the office card, outside its fixed-bottom container — confirmed for the seventh consecutive review. Carlos barely visible at top-right desk on mobile. Footer wraps onto 2 lines at 375px.

carries_still_broken.shortcut_toast_mobile: CONFIRMED AGAIN — 7th consecutive review. Mobile screenshot at 375x812 shows toast text rendered as plain text BELOW the office card, outside the fixed-bottom container. Zero lines of CSS or JS have changed to address this.
carries_still_broken.conditional_aria_live_mounts: Four instances still conditionally mount with content already set: (1) completionToast App.jsx ~955, (2) plantToast Office.jsx ~935, (3) ShortcutToast div App.jsx ~543, (4) polling-paused-pill App.jsx ~1007. All four fail NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver announcement. Unchanged.
carries_still_broken.stable_activity_log_noop: App.jsx line 759: useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog]) still a no-op. WorkerCard memo defeated on every 30s poll across all 4 call sites. Unchanged.
carries_still_broken.office_jsx_monolith: Office.jsx is now 1245 lines — grown 27 lines since last flagged at 1218. Going the wrong direction.
carries_still_broken.activityLogLenRef_dead_code: App.jsx lines 758–760: useRef set, never read. Still there.
carries_still_broken.ghost_chars_tabindex: Office.jsx line 525: tabIndex={0} unconditional on all Character divs including ghost variant. Ghost has no onClick, no role=button, no onKeyDown. Keyboard dead-end. Unchanged.
carries_still_broken.wander_cap_at_4: Office.jsx line 1159: (i % 4) + 1. 5th idle worker duplicates wander-1 path and tooltip. Unchanged.
carries_still_broken.stuck_card_keyboard: App.jsx lines 426–440: stat-card--stuck div has onClick but no role, no tabIndex, no onKeyDown. Not keyboard accessible.
carries_still_broken.flip_focus_management: WorkerCard flip to info view does not move focus to Close button. Keyboard users navigate blind.
carries_still_broken.role_radio_on_button: App.jsx lines 1153–1162 and 1165–1183: <button role='radio'> conflicting ARIA. Unchanged.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast on mobile — CONFIRMED for the 7th consecutive review
suggestions.description: Fresh mobile screenshot at 375x812 confirms the toast renders as plain unstyled text below the office card, outside its fixed-bottom container. The text '1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' floats below the office floor with no background, no border, no positioning — it has completely escaped its CSS. This has been photographed and described in every single review since #13. The fix is one line of CSS: `@media (hover: none) and (pointer: coarse) { .shortcut-toast { display: none; } }` — keyboard shortcuts are meaningless on touch-only devices. If you want to be thorough, add a JS guard in ShortcutToast: `if (window.matchMedia('(hover: none) and (pointer: coarse)').matches) return null` at the top of the component. Either way: 7 reviews, still zero action.

suggestions.title: Four conditional aria-live mounts — all still broken, all unchanged
suggestions.description:
A persistent aria-live region must exist in the DOM BEFORE content is set — conditionally mounting an element with content already inside it bypasses the mutation observer that screen readers use. All four instances: (1) completionToast (App.jsx ~955), (2) plantToast (Office.jsx ~935), (3) ShortcutToast div (App.jsx ~543), (4) polling-paused-pill span (App.jsx ~1007). Fix once, fix all: add `
` to App root unconditionally, then write a `useAnnounce()` hook that imperatively sets its textContent. All four toast/notification triggers call announce() instead of conditional mounting. One hook, four WCAG 4.1.3 violations resolved.

suggestions.title: stableActivityLog useMemo is still a no-op — memo defeat on every poll
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 759: `const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog])`. The dependency IS the state value — useMemo runs whenever activityLog changes, producing a new reference. Since activityLog is replaced wholesale on every syncFromGitHub call (every 30s), stableActivityLog produces a new reference every 30s. All four WorkerCard render sites pass this as activityEntries prop. React.memo does shallow compare: new reference = re-render. Every WorkerCard re-renders every 30s unconditionally. Fix: `useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog.length])` — only produces new reference when entry count changes, not on identity replacement. One-word change. This was flagged in reviews #15 through #19.

suggestions.title: NEW: Luna SVG gradient ID collision — visual rendering bug with multiple Luna instances
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 252: `const gradId = 'luna-hair-${workerId}'` evaluates to the hardcoded string 'luna-hair-luna' every time, because workerId is always 'luna' for this branch. SVG gradient IDs must be globally unique in the DOM. When Luna appears simultaneously as a desk character (working/ghost variant at a desk) AND is also rendered in the ConferenceTable's meetingWorkers map (char--seated), or in CharacterAvatar used in the hover card, two SVG elements exist with `id='luna-hair-luna'`. Browsers use the first matching ID — Luna's hair gradient in the second render instance will display incorrectly (wrong color or fallback to black). Fix: use React's `useId()` hook, or generate a unique suffix per render (e.g., pass a `instanceId` prop, or use `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)` stored in a ref). The ConferenceTable renders at size={16} so this collision is observable when a huddle meeting is active.

suggestions.title: Office.jsx now 1245 lines — grew between reviews, not shrinking
suggestions.description: The file was 1218 lines when first flagged, now 1245. It's growing. Concrete extraction plan: (1) `src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx` — ~250 lines of per-worker SVG art, pure memo, no office state. (2) `src/components/WallClock.jsx` — ~55 lines. (3) `src/components/ConferenceTable.jsx` — ~95 lines. (4) `src/components/VibeShockwave.jsx` — ~30 lines. (5) `src/components/AwaySign.jsx` — ~35 lines. (6) Move `formatIdleDuration` + `AWAY_MESSAGES` to `src/utils/time.js`. Extracting CharacterAvatar alone reduces Office.jsx by 250 lines to ~995. At minimum, extract CharacterAvatar — it has zero dependencies on office-level state.

suggestions.title: Character keyboard handler still calls handleClick() without event — TypeError on touch+keyboard
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 523: `handleClick()` invoked with no arguments in the onKeyDown handler. handleClick (lines 506–513) immediately calls `e.stopPropagation()` when `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches`. On a device where hover:none is true AND an external keyboard is attached (iPad + Bluetooth keyboard, Surface, Chromebook), pressing Enter/Space on a character calls handleClick(undefined), then undefined.stopPropagation() → TypeError. One character fix: `handleClick(e)` instead of `handleClick()`.

suggestions.title: NEW: ConferenceTable meeting-worker positions use cx instead of cy for Y-axis calculation
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 733: `const y = cx - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)` — uses `cx` (the X-center, value 52) where `cy` (the Y-center, also value 52) should be used. This is a copy-paste error from the outer chair-placement loop (line 691: `const y = cy - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)`). Currently benign because cx === cy === 52, so the result is numerically identical. But if the table were ever repositioned to a non-square center (e.g., cx=60, cy=52 for alignment reasons), the Huddle meeting-worker avatars would render at incorrect Y positions. Fix: declare both `cx` and `cy` in the inner scope, or reference `cy` from outer scope: `const { cx, cy } = { cx: 52, cy: 52 }`.

suggestions.title: Ghost characters still keyboard-focusable with no action — tabIndex should be conditional
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 525: `tabIndex={0}` on every Character div regardless of variant. Ghost-variant characters have no onClick (handleClick is undefined when !onWorkerClick), no role='button', no useful keyboard behavior. A keyboard user tabs into a ghost desk character, an aria-label is announced, Enter/Space does nothing. Fix: `tabIndex={handleClick ? 0 : -1}`. Ghost characters still display ghostBubble on focus (handleMouseEnter fires for ghost on line 456), so focus feedback exists — but the tab stop should be opt-in.

suggestions.title: NEW: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and AWAY_MESSAGES defined inside component scope — recreated each render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 390: `const IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS = { 1: '☕', 2: '💬', 3: '🪟', 4: '🧘' }` is defined inside the Character component body. It's a static constant with no dependency on props or state — it should be a module-level constant. Every render of every Character instance (there can be 5) allocates and GCs this object. Same applies to `getHour` (a static arrow function) defined inside WindowElement at line 603. Neither object needs to be inside the component. Move both outside their respective component functions.

suggestions.title: DeltaBadge renders ↑/↓ arrows with no accessible label
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 364–371: DeltaBadge renders `↑3` or `↓2` as raw text. Screen readers announce '↑' as 'up arrow', leaving users to guess 'the active count went up by 3'. Add `aria-label={`${delta > 0 ? 'Increased' : 'Decreased'} by ${Math.abs(delta)}`}` to the span, and `aria-hidden='true'` to the directional glyph.

suggestions.title: Stuck worker stat card click-only — keyboard users locked out of scroll-to-worker
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 426–440: stat-card--stuck is a bare `<div>` with onClick. No role, no tabIndex, no onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot navigate to the stuck worker from the stats bar. Fix: role='button', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown that calls the same scroll handler on Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: Wander animation capped at 4 — 5th idle worker duplicates path and tooltip
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1159: `(i % 4) + 1`. With 5 or more idle workers, index 4 wraps to wander-1 — same keyframe path, same ☕ idle-bubble, same tooltip 'Heading to the coffee corner'. Two characters animate in lockstep. Feels buggy. Add char--wander-5 keyframe and a 5th wanderTooltips entry.

suggestions.title: vibeHistory sparkline lazy-loads from localStorage — blank on first render for returning users
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 581: `useState([])`. On every page load the sparkline is empty until the first poll's teamVibe.key effect runs (~2–30s depending on network). The data is in localStorage already. Fix: lazy initializer `useState(() => { try { const r = localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-history'); return r ? JSON.parse(r) : [] } catch { return [] } })`.

suggestions.title: activityLogLenRef is dead code — written, never read
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 758–760: `const activityLogLenRef = useRef(0)` and a useEffect that updates it. The ref is never read anywhere in 1209 lines. Delete both lines.

suggestions.title: formatIdleDuration / formatDuration / getRelativeTime — three near-identical time formatters in two files
suggestions.description: Office.jsx defines formatIdleDuration (~318–327). App.jsx defines getRelativeTime (~28) and formatDuration (~215). All three compute elapsed time from a timestamp with slightly different output strings. None live in src/utils/. This is textbook divergent drift — next time someone changes the 'hour threshold', they'll update one and break the others. Move to src/utils/time.js with a shared base.

suggestions.title: role='radio' on <button> elements — conflicting ARIA semantics
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1153–1162 and 1165–1183: `<button role='radio' aria-checked={...}>`. Screen readers may announce 'radio button button'. The correct pattern for mutually-exclusive filters: use role='tab'/role='tablist', or switch to `<input type='radio'>` with labels. The outer radiogroup div is correct; the button element itself is the conflict.

suggestions.title: Worker card flip does not move focus to Close button
suggestions.description: WorkerCard: clicking the info (ℹ️) button flips to the back, but focus stays on the now-hidden front button. Keyboard users must Tab-hunt to find Close. Add a useEffect that calls focus() on the back-close button ref when isFlipped becomes true.

suggestions.title: 19 continuously-animating elements lack will-change — GPU hint missing
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders 12 ambient-particle spans; Office.jsx renders 7 more. All animate with no `will-change: transform`. Plus char--wander-* characters animate indefinitely during idle. Add `will-change: transform` to .ambient-particle and .char--wander-1 through -4 (soon -5) in CSS. Negligible on desktop, real jank on mid-range mobile.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar receives unused 'updatedAt' prop
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 166: updatedAt is destructured but never referenced inside ProgressBar — ETA uses startedAt. Every call site passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at}. Dead prop. Either remove from signature and all call sites, or use it as a fallback when startedAt is absent.

whats_good: Live site healthy — fully rendered in under 4s, no console errors, no blank page. Puppeteer domcontentloaded navigation clean.

Crushing It vibe fully active: COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label on coffee corner, CVO nameplate on Bloberto, 🏆 trophy on manager desk, full 6-chair conference table. Vibe-reactive theming is consistently executed across every UI surface.
Per-worker desk screen SVGs (Carlos: code lines, Maya: wireframe grid, Dave: blinking cursor, Sofia: bar chart, Luna: color blocks) are impressive craft. All correctly aria-hidden='true'.
Deep-work detection (>45 min at desk triggers char--deep-work gold glow + 🎧) is a smart personality-adding heuristic with no UX noise.
Favicon dynamic color-coding (canvas, no external deps) silently works in the background — professional touch.
AwaySign folded-corner sticky-note SVG with seed-based message rotation and data-tier coloring is genuinely charming detail.
Polling pause on 10-min inactivity with 15s check interval, resumed on any interaction — well-implemented. The polling-paused-pill UX is clear even if the aria-live on it is broken.
Reduce-motion coverage is comprehensive throughout both CSS files.
summary: Score drops from 3.4 to 3.3. Two new bugs found: (1) Luna SVG gradient ID collision — the hardcoded 'luna-hair-luna' ID causes incorrect gradient rendering when Luna appears in multiple CharacterAvatar instances simultaneously (desk + huddle). (2) ConferenceTable meeting-worker Y-position uses cx instead of cy — currently harmless since cx === cy === 52, but a copy-paste error waiting to bite. The ShortcutToast is now confirmed broken for the seventh consecutive review — at this point it's not a bug, it's a feature of this codebase. The four aria-live antipatterns, stableActivityLog no-op, and Office.jsx growing to 1245 lines remain completely unaddressed. The window element blown-out white rectangle is visible in every screenshot yet remains unfixed. The visual craft and fun factor remain excellent — the COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label, the vibe-reactive nameplate changes, the per-worker desk screens — this is a genuinely delightful product buried under an increasingly long and embarrassingly fixable backlog.
